name: app.R
content:
# Complete randomization simulator (2-arm)
# app.R
library(shiny)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(DT)

# Helper: simulate one trial
sim_trial <- function(N, p,
                      prob_male = 0.5,
                      prob_old = 0.5,
                      prob_advanced = 0.5,
                      seed = 123142) {
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  dat <- tibble(
    id = seq_len(N),
    treatment = ifelse(rbinom(N, size = 1, prob = p) == 1, "A", "B"),
    gender = ifelse(rbinom(N, 1, prob_male) == 1, "Male", "Female"),
    age    = ifelse(rbinom(N, 1, prob_old) == 1, "Old", "Young"),
    stage  = ifelse(rbinom(N, 1, prob_advanced) == 1, "Advanced", "Early")
  ) %>%
    mutate(cum_A = cumsum(treatment == "A"),
           cum_prop_A = cum_A / id)
  attr(dat, "p") <- p
  dat
}

# Metrics for covariate balance (standardized difference for binary covariates)
cov_balance_metrics <- function(dat) {
  # compute proportion of level within each arm
  covs <- c("gender", "age", "stage")
  out <- tibble()
  for (v in covs) {
    tab <- dat %>%
      group_by(treatment, !!sym(v)) %>%
      summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
      group_by(treatment) %>%
      mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
      ungroup()
    
    # choose a reference level to compute difference (level 1)
    levels_v <- unique(dat[[v]])
    lvl1 <- levels_v[1]
    pA <- tab %>% filter(treatment == "A", !!sym(v) == lvl1) %>% pull(prop)
    pB <- tab %>% filter(treatment == "B", !!sym(v) == lvl1) %>% pull(prop)
    pA <- ifelse(length(pA) == 0, 0, pA)
    pB <- ifelse(length(pB) == 0, 0, pB)
    
    # pooled SD for binary prop: sqrt(p*(1-p))
    p_pool <- (pA + pB) / 2
    sd_pool <- sqrt(p_pool * (1 - p_pool))
    std_diff <- ifelse(sd_pool > 0, (pA - pB) / sd_pool, NA_real_)
    
    out <- bind_rows(
      out,
      tibble(
        covariate = v,
        level = lvl1,
        pA = pA,
        pB = pB,
        std_diff = std_diff
      )
    )
  }
  out
}

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Complete randomization simulator (2-arm)"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      numericInput("N", "Number of participants (N):", value = 20, min = 2, step = 1),
      sliderInput("p", "Prob(Treatment = A) (p):", min = 0.01, max = 0.99, value = 0.5, step = 0.01),
      hr(),
      h5("Covariate marginal probabilities"),
      sliderInput("pmale", "Pr(Male):", min = 0, max = 1, value = 0.5, step = 0.05),
      sliderInput("pold", "Pr(Old):", min = 0, max = 1, value = 0.5, step = 0.05),
      sliderInput("padv", "Pr(Advanced stage):", min = 0, max = 1, value = 0.5, step = 0.05),
      hr(),
      numericInput("seed", "Random seed (leave 0 for random):", value = 123142, min = 0, step = 1),
      actionButton("sim", "Simulate one trial"),
      br(), br(),
      numericInput("nsim", "Number of replicate trials (M):", value = 100, min = 1, step = 1),
      actionButton("runsim", "Run Monte Carlo"),
      br(), br(),
      downloadButton("download_data", "Download current simulated data (CSV)")
    ),
    mainPanel(
      tabsetPanel(
        id = "main_tabs",
        tabPanel(
          "Single trial",
          fluidRow(
            column(6, plotOutput("alloc_bar")),
            column(6, plotOutput("cum_prop"))
          ),
          hr(),
          fluidRow(
            column(6, plotOutput("cov_by_arm")),
            column(
              6,
              tags$h4("Covariate balance table"),
              DTOutput("cov_balance_table")
            )
          ),
          hr(),
          tags$h4("Participant table"),
          DTOutput("sim_table")
        ),
        tabPanel(
          "Monte Carlo",
          fluidRow(
            column(6, plotOutput("hist_propA")),
            column(6, plotOutput("hist_maxStdDiff"))
          ),
          hr(),
          tags$h4("Monte Carlo summary table"),
          DTOutput("mc_summary")
        ),
        tabPanel(
          "How to use",
          tags$div(
            style = "max-width: 900px;",
            tags$h4("Purpose of this simulator"),
            tags$p(
              "This app demonstrates complete randomization in a two-arm trial. ",
              "Each participant is assigned independently to Treatment A with probability p ",
              "and to Treatment B with probability 1 - p. Complete randomization is highly ",
              "unpredictable, but it does not guarantee balanced treatment groups or balanced ",
              "baseline covariates in any single trial."
            ),
            tags$p(
              "Use the app to explore the essential trade-off: complete randomization balances ",
              "groups on average across repeated trials, while noticeable imbalance can still ",
              "occur by chance, especially when the sample size is small."
            ),
            tags$hr(),
            tags$h4("1. Set up the trial"),
            tags$ul(
              tags$li(
                "Number of participants (N): choose the planned sample size. Smaller trials usually show more visible ",
                "random imbalance."
              ),
              tags$li(
                "Prob(Treatment = A) (p): set the target allocation probability. For equal allocation, use p = 0.50."
              ),
              tags$li(
                "Covariate marginal probabilities: set how common Male, Old, and Advanced stage participants are in the simulated ",
                "study population. These covariates are generated independently and are not used ",
                "to assign treatment."
              ),
              tags$li(
                "Random seed: use the same positive seed to reproduce the same simulation. Enter 0 when you ",
                "want a fresh random result each time."
              )
            ),
            tags$h4("2. Simulate one trial"),
            tags$p(
              "Click ",
              "Simulate one trial",
              " and open the ",
              "Single trial",
              " tab. This shows one possible realization of the design."
            ),
            tags$ul(
              tags$li(
                "Final allocation counts: the number of participants assigned to A and B. Even with p = 0.50, the counts ",
                "may not be equal."
              ),
              tags$li(
                "Cumulative allocation proportion: how the proportion assigned to A changes over enrollment. The dashed red line ",
                "marks the target probability p."
              ),
              tags$li(
                "Covariate distribution by arm: whether gender, age, and stage look similar across treatment arms in this one ",
                "realized trial."
              ),
              tags$li(
                "Covariate balance table: standardized differences summarize covariate imbalance. They are descriptive ",
                "measures of chance imbalance, not a test of whether randomization was valid."
              ),
              tags$li(
                "Participant table: the simulated assignment and covariates for each participant. Use the download ",
                "button if you want the current data as a CSV file."
              ),
              tags$li(
                "Table summaries: use the covariate balance table together with the participant table to connect ",
                "the numerical balance measures back to the individual simulated participants."
              )
            ),
            tags$h4("3. Run repeated trials"),
            tags$p(
              "Set ",
              "Number of replicate trials (M)",
              " and either click ",
              "Run Monte Carlo",
              " or open the ",
              "Monte Carlo",
              " tab. The app repeats the same trial design many times while keeping N, p, and the ",
              "covariate probabilities fixed."
            ),
            tags$ul(
              tags$li(
                "Distribution of final prop(A): shows how much the final allocation proportion varies across repeated trials. ",
                "The dashed red line is the target probability."
              ),
              tags$li(
                "Distribution of maximum covariate imbalance: shows the largest absolute standardized difference among the simulated ",
                "covariates in each repeated trial."
              ),
              tags$li(
                "Summary table: reports the average and standard deviation of the allocation proportion and ",
                "maximum covariate imbalance across the repeated trials. Use it to compare settings such as ",
                "small versus large N, or equal versus unequal allocation probabilities."
              )
            ),
            tags$h4("4. Things to try"),
            tags$ul(
              tags$li("Start with N = 20, p = 0.50, and M = 100. Notice how often the arms are not exactly balanced."),
              tags$li("Increase N to 200 and rerun the Monte Carlo simulation. The spread should become smaller."),
              tags$li("Change p away from 0.50 to explore unequal allocation ratios."),
              tags$li("Change the covariate probabilities and check whether chance covariate imbalance still appears."),
              tags$li("Compare several seeds to see how much a single realized trial can differ from another.")
            ),
            tags$h4("Key interpretation"),
            tags$p(
              "Complete randomization is simple and protects unpredictability because each ",
              "assignment is independent. Its limitation is finite-sample balance: treatment ",
              "counts and covariates may be uneven in a particular trial, even when the ",
              "procedure is working correctly. Monte Carlo simulation helps you see what kinds ",
              "of imbalance are plausible under the design before choosing whether a more ",
              "restricted or covariate-adaptive procedure is needed."
            )
          )
        ),
        tabPanel(
          "Cite",
          tags$h4("Cite this software"),
          tags$p("Please cite this software in publications or presentations that use it:"),
          tags$p(
            "Tamasi, Katalin. Complete randomization simulator (2-arm), version 1.0. University Medical Center Groningen, 2025. Shiny application. ",
            tags$a(
              href = "https://katatam.github.io/randomization/complete_rand/",
              "https://katatam.github.io/randomization/complete_rand/",
              target = "_blank"
            )
          ),
          hr(),
          tags$p("\u00A9 2025 Katalin Tamasi. This software is licensed under the MIT License. You may use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the software, subject to the terms of the MIT License.")
        )
      )
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  # reactive simulated single trial
  sim_dat <- eventReactive(input$sim, {
    seed <- if (input$seed > 0) input$seed else NULL
    sim_trial(
      input$N, input$p,
      prob_male = input$pmale,
      prob_old = input$pold,
      prob_advanced = input$padv,
      seed = seed
    )
  }, ignoreNULL = FALSE) # run initially with defaults
  
  output$alloc_bar <- renderPlot({
    dat <- sim_dat()
    agg <- dat %>% count(treatment)
    ggplot(agg, aes(x = treatment, y = n, fill = treatment)) +
      geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
      labs(x = "Treatment", y = "N", title = "Final allocation counts") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
  })
  
  output$cum_prop <- renderPlot({
    dat <- sim_dat()
    ggplot(dat, aes(x = id, y = cum_prop_A)) +
      geom_step() +
      geom_hline(yintercept = attr(dat, "p"), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
      labs(
        x = "Enrollment order (participant)",
        y = "Cumulative proportion assigned to A",
        title = "Cumulative allocation proportion (A)"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
  })
  
  output$cov_by_arm <- renderPlot({
    dat <- sim_dat()
    long <- dat %>%
      pivot_longer(cols = c(gender, age, stage), names_to = "cov", values_to = "level")
    ggplot(long, aes(x = treatment, fill = level)) +
      geom_bar(position = "fill") +
      facet_wrap(~cov) +
      labs(y = "Proportion", title = "Covariate distribution by arm") +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
  })
  
  # Covariate balance table
  output$cov_balance_table <- renderDT({
    dat <- sim_dat()
    bal <- cov_balance_metrics(dat)
    datatable(
      bal,
      rownames = FALSE,
      colnames = c(
        "Covariate",
        "Reference level",
        "Proportion in A",
        "Proportion in B",
        "Standardized difference"
      ),
      options = list(dom = "t")
    ) %>%
      formatRound(columns = 3:ncol(bal), digits = 2)
  })
  
  # Simulated participant table
  output$sim_table <- renderDT({
    dat <- sim_dat()
    datatable(dat, options = list(pageLength = 10)) %>%
      formatRound(columns = which(sapply(dat, is.numeric)), digits = 2)
  })
  
  output$download_data <- downloadHandler(
    filename = function() {
      paste0("sim_trial_", Sys.Date(), ".csv")
    },
    content = function(file) {
      write.csv(sim_dat(), file, row.names = FALSE)
    }
  )
  
  # Monte Carlo
  mc_trigger <- reactiveVal(0)
  
  observeEvent(input$runsim, {
    mc_trigger(mc_trigger() + 1)
  })
  
  observeEvent(input$main_tabs, {
    if (identical(input$main_tabs, "Monte Carlo")) {
      mc_trigger(mc_trigger() + 1)
    }
  }, ignoreInit = TRUE)
  
  mc_res <- eventReactive(mc_trigger(), {
    M <- input$nsim
    N <- input$N
    p <- input$p
    pmale <- input$pmale
    pold <- input$pold
    padv <- input$padv
    seed0 <- if (input$seed > 0) input$seed else NULL
    
    # simple loop; for large M you might want future / parallel
    propsA <- numeric(M)
    maxStdDiff <- numeric(M)
    sd_by_cov <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = M, ncol = 3)
    colnames(sd_by_cov) <- c("gender", "age", "stage")
    
    for (i in seq_len(M)) {
      sdat <- sim_trial(
        N, p,
        prob_male = pmale,
        prob_old = pold,
        prob_advanced = padv,
        seed = if (is.null(seed0)) NULL else seed0 + i
      )
      propsA[i] <- mean(sdat$treatment == "A")
      bal <- cov_balance_metrics(sdat)
      
      # absolute standardized differences
      abs_sd <- abs(bal$std_diff)
      
      # replace NA with 0 if p_pool 0 or 1
      abs_sd[is.na(abs_sd)] <- 0
      maxStdDiff[i] <- max(abs_sd)
      sd_by_cov[i, ] <- abs_sd
    }
    
    list(
      propsA = propsA,
      maxStdDiff = maxStdDiff,
      sd_by_cov = sd_by_cov,
      M = M,
      p = p
    )
  })
  
  output$hist_propA <- renderPlot({
    res <- mc_res()
    if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)
    df <- data.frame(propA = res$propsA)
    ggplot(df, aes(x = propA)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 30, color = "white", fill = "steelblue") +
      geom_vline(xintercept = res$p, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
      labs(
        x = "Final proportion assigned to A",
        y = "Count",
        title = paste("Distribution of final prop(A) over", res$M, "simulations")
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
  })
  
  output$hist_maxStdDiff <- renderPlot({
    res <- mc_res()
    if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)
    df <- data.frame(maxStdDiff = res$maxStdDiff)
    ggplot(df, aes(x = maxStdDiff)) +
      geom_histogram(bins = 30, color = "white", fill = "coral") +
      labs(
        x = "Maximum absolute standardized difference (covariates)",
        y = "Count",
        title = "Distribution of maximum covariate imbalance across simulations"
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
  })
  
  # Monte Carlo summary
  output$mc_summary <- renderDT({
    res <- mc_res()
    if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)
    df <- tibble(
      measure = c(
        "Mean final proportion assigned to A",
        "SD final proportion assigned to A",
        "Mean maximum absolute standardized difference",
        "SD maximum absolute standardized difference"
      ),
      value = c(
        mean(res$propsA), sd(res$propsA),
        mean(res$maxStdDiff), sd(res$maxStdDiff)
      )
    )
    datatable(
      df,
      colnames = c("Measure", "Value"),
      options = list(dom = "t"),
      rownames = FALSE
    ) %>%
      formatRound(columns = 2, digits = 2)
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
